trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade. Turkey exports vehicles, machinery, textiles, and food products to the US; the US exports aircraft, defence equipment, chemicals, and machinery to Turkey. Trade has been affected by tariff disputes (2018 steel/aluminium tariffs) but has largely recovered.
disputes: 2018 US steel and aluminium tariffs on Turkey (partially resolved)
CAATSA sanctions threat over S-400 purchase

diplomatic.summary: Turkey and the US are formal NATO allies but have experienced significant strains over multiple issues: Turkey's purchase of the Russian S-400 air defence system, which led to Turkey's removal from the F-35 programme; diverging positions on Kurdish forces in Syria; Turkey's partial accommodation of Russia despite NATO membership; and President Erdogan's adversarial relationship with successive US administrations. The alliance persists but trust has significantly eroded.
recent_incidents: Turkey removed from F-35 programme after S-400 purchase (2019)
US imposed CAATSA sanctions on Turkey's defence procurement agency (2020)
Turkey's blocking of Sweden and Finland's initial NATO applications (2022-2024)
Divergence on Syria policy regarding Kurdish YPG/SDF forces

military.summary: Formal NATO allies with Turkey hosting the Incirlik Air Base — a critical NATO asset — and serving as guardian of the Turkish Straits. However, Turkey's S-400 purchase led to its expulsion from the F-35 programme and CAATSA sanctions. Military cooperation continues through NATO frameworks but bilateral trust is damaged. Turkey is the only NATO member that refused to apply Russia sanctions unanimously. Joint exercises continue under NATO auspices.

financial.summary: Moderate financial ties. US firms have significant Turkey investments in manufacturing, finance, and energy. Turkey holds US Treasury bonds as part of FX reserves. The Turkish lira crisis of 2018 was partly triggered by US-Turkey political tensions. US investors maintain exposure through emerging market funds.

energy.summary: Growing US LNG exports to Turkey as Turkey diversifies away from Russian gas post-2022. US energy firms are active in Turkey's upstream sector. Turkey's role as an energy transit hub is strategically important to the US. Turkey participates in the Southern Gas Corridor favoured by the US.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific collaboration through academic exchanges, Fulbright programmes, and joint research. Turkish universities have strong US partnerships. Bilateral STEM collaboration has continued despite political tensions. Brain drain of Turkish scientists to the US has increased.
